info.description:
## IS 456:2000 Compliant Structural Engineering Library

This API provides comprehensive structural engineering calculations following
the Indian Standard IS 456:2000 for reinforced concrete design.

### Features

- **Beam Design**: Flexure, shear, and combined design calculations
- **Detailing**: Reinforcement layout, spacing, and development lengths
- **Optimization**: Cost-optimized beam cross-section selection
- **Smart Analysis**: AI-assisted design suggestions and insights
- **3D Geometry**: Visualization-ready mesh generation

### Design Codes

- IS 456:2000 - Plain and Reinforced Concrete
- IS 13920 - Ductile Detailing for Seismic Resistance

### Units

All inputs and outputs use consistent units:
- **Length**: millimeters (mm)
- **Force**: kilonewtons (kN)
- **Moment**: kilonewton-meters (kN·m)
- **Stress**: N/mm² (MPa)
- **Area**: mm²
info.title: Structural Engineering API

get.description:
Root endpoint providing API information.

Returns basic API information and links to documentation.

post.description: Get AI-assisted analysis with suggestions for beam design.

get.description: Get IS 456 clause references for common checks.

post.description: Compute BMD and SFD for a beam with UDL and/or point loads. Returns discretized diagrams + critical points (max moment, max shear).
Calculate required reinforcement for a beam section under given loading.
Check if a beam with given reinforcement is adequate for the applied loads.
Run a multi-case IS 456 compliance report. Checks flexure, shear, deflection, and crack width for each load case and identifies the governing case.
Check crack width using an Annex-F-style estimate per IS 456:2000. Computes estimated crack width and compares against exposure-class limits.
Check deflection using span/depth ratio (Level A) per IS 456:2000 Cl 23.2. Compares actual L/d against allowable ratios with modification factors.
Run IS 13920 beam ductility checks for a single section. Checks geometry (Cl 6.1), min/max steel (Cl 6.2), and confinement spacing (Cl 6.3.5).
Calculate enhanced design shear strength τc' for sections close to supports per IS 456:2000 Cl 40.3. Applies when a concentrated load acts within 2d of the face of support.
Check beam slenderness for lateral stability per IS 456:2000 Cl 23.3. Verifies slenderness ratio l_eff/b against code limits.
Design a beam for combined torsion, shear, and bending per IS 456 Cl 41.
Complete column design check — classifies the column, computes effective length, applies minimum eccentricity, checks axial and bending capacity per the appropriate IS 456 clause.
Calculate additional moment Ma = Pu × eadd for slender columns, where eadd = D × (le/D)² / 2000. Includes k-factor reduction per Cl 39.7.1.1.
post.summary: Additional Moment for Slender Columns per IS 456 Cl 39.7.1

post.description: Calculate the axial load capacity of a short column under pure axial load (or minimum eccentricity) per IS 456:2000 Cl. 39.3: Pu = 0.4·fck·Ac + 0.67·fy·Asc.
Check a column section under biaxial bending using the Bresler load contour method per IS 456:2000 Cl. 39.6. Returns the interaction ratio (Mux/Mux1)^αn + (Muy/Muy1)^αn and safety status.
post.summary: Biaxial Bending Check per IS 456 Cl 39.6

post.description: Classify a column as SHORT or SLENDER based on its slenderness ratio le/D per IS 456:2000 Cl. 25.1.2. Short columns have le/D < 12.
Check longitudinal bar limits, tie sizing, spacing, and cross-tie requirements for a column section per IS 456:2000 Cl. 26.5.3.
Check column ductile detailing per IS 13920:2016 Cl 7. Validates geometry, longitudinal steel limits, special confining reinforcement spacing, confinement zone length, and confining bar area.
Calculate the minimum eccentricity for a column per IS 456:2000 Cl. 25.4: e_min = max(l/500 + D/30, 20 mm).
Calculate the effective length of a column based on end restraint conditions per IS 456:2000 Cl. 25.2, Table 28. Returns le = ratio × l for seven standard end-condition cases.
Check helical reinforcement adequacy for circular columns. Verifies pitch limits and computes the 1.05 enhancement factor per IS 456 Cl 39.4.
post.summary: Helical Reinforcement Check per IS 456 Cl 39.4

post.description: Generate the P-M interaction diagram for a rectangular column section per IS 456:2000 Cl. 39.5. Returns the full curve with key points (pure axial, balanced, pure bending).
Design a slender column with augmented moments for P-delta effects. Classifies each axis, computes additional eccentricities, applies k-factor reduction, and checks biaxial interaction.
post.summary: Long (Slender) Column Design per IS 456 Cl 39.7

post.description: Design a short column for uniaxial bending per IS 456:2000 Cl. 39.5. Generates the P-M interaction envelope and checks whether the applied (Pu, Mu) lies within it.

get.description: Get IS 456 design limits and constraints.

post.description: Check anchorage of bottom bars at simple supports per IS 456:2000 Cl 26.2.3.3. Verifies that development length is adequate beyond the face of support.
post.summary: Anchorage Check at Simple Support (IS 456 Cl 26.2.3.3)

get.description: Get cross-sectional areas for standard reinforcement bars.

post.description: Generate reinforcement detailing for a beam section.

get.description: Calculate development length for a specific bar diameter.

post.description: Generate BBS from beam parameters and return as CSV download.
Generate a summary report for all beams in a building (HTML, PDF, or CSV).
Generate beam DXF drawing and return as download.
Generate a design report in HTML or JSON format.
Generate 3D mesh geometry for beam visualization.

Generate complete 3D geometry with rebars and stirrups using structural_lib.

This endpoint returns rich geometry data including:
- Concrete outline (8 corner points)
- Rebar paths with segments (position, diameter, length)
- Stirrup loops with corner positions

Ideal for React Three Fiber rendering with cylinder/tube primitives.

Generate 3D line geometry for a multi-beam building view.

Returns line segments for all beams/columns suitable for instanced rendering.
Ideal for React Three Fiber with LineSegments or Tubes.

Generate 2D cross-section geometry for beam section view.

Returns outline, rebar positions, and stirrup path for visualization.
Ideal for React SVG or Canvas 2D rendering.

Validates first, then computes:
- Rebar positions and paths
- Stirrup positions and loops
- Steel area provided

Returns geometry suitable for React Three Fiber rendering.

Use this before applying configuration to check for issues.
